Fixing dead slider warnings by ensuring wingSpan and tailHeight are properly referenced in formulas, and adding cockpit to the final compound. The fuselage loft chain should produce visible body - verifying curve connections are correct.

PolylineCurve nodes fail because they receive only 1 point each - need 4+ points for closed wing profiles. Replacing wing/tail construction with Box primitives + Rotate transforms for reliability. Also ensuring wingSpan and tailHeight sliders are properly referenced.

Building a simple but complete Boeing 747 using reliable primitives (Cylinder for fuselage, Box for wings/tail, Cylinder for engines). All parts anchored to fuselage center point. Using 5 sliders for parametric control.

Issues detected (auto-repair 1/2): Dropped invalid edge: Node "wingRotateL" has invalid data: unknown parameter "pivot" on node "wingRotateL" (node type "Rotate" does not support "pivot"). Valid parameters: angle, axisX, axisY, axisZ, isLocal; Dropped invalid edge: Node "wingRotateR" has invalid data: unknown parameter "pivot" on node "wingRotateR" (node type "Rotate" does not support "pivot"). Valid parameters: angle, axisX, axisY, axisZ, isLocal; Dropped invalid edge: fuseRotate → noseCone ("Cone" has no input accepting what "fuseRotate" outputs (inputs: center, axis; numeric params need targetHandle "param:<name>"))

Fixing critical issues: Rotate has no pivot param (use Point centers instead), nose/tail cones at origin need proper X positions, hStabL/R and engines occupy same space need Y offsets, cockpit detached. Rebuilding with proper positioning via Point centers and parametric formulas.

Engine rotate nodes need solid input. Connecting engineL→engineLRotate and engineR→engineRRotate to complete the transform chains. The rotated engines will be the final leaves.
